title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: Les sons du français
days.objective: Learn the French sounds that spelling hides, so you can read and hear French correctly.
days.paretoFocus: French isn't phonetic like it looks: master the nasal vowels, silent endings and a few accents once, and pronunciation stops being a mystery.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 sound/pronunciation activity.
Repeat the nasal vowels on/an/in aloud.
Read 10 words aloud from the cheatsheet.

items: Say the three nasal vowels aloud.
Recall: is a final consonant usually pronounced?
Read 'bonjour, ça va ?' with correct sounds.

activities.title: Les sons clés
activities.intro: French letters combine into sounds. The big three to master are the nasal vowels (air through the nose) and the silent final consonants. Say each aloud.

items.en: final consonants are usually silent: peti(t), vou(s)

activities.title: Les sons
activities.intro: Choose or complete.

questions.prompt: Which word contains a NASAL vowel?
questions.explanation: 'ain' in pain is a nasal vowel; the others are oral.

questions.prompt: The accent in 'été' is called…

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Very well, thank you.

days.title: Bonjour et politesse
days.objective: Greet, thank and say goodbye politely in both registers.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of fixed politeness formulas cover most first contacts. Learn the tu/vous difference now.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'greetings' activity.
Practise tu vs vous with 5 example people.
Review today's words in the Words tab.

items: Recall bonjour / bonsoir / salut and when to use each.
Say 's'il vous plaît' and 's'il te plaît'.
Reply to 'Merci !'.

activities.title: Formules de politesse
activities.intro: French politeness is a fixed set of phrases. 'vous' is formal/plural; 'tu' is informal.

questions.prompt: You greet your boss in the morning:
questions.explanation: Bonjour is the standard, polite daytime greeting.

days.title: Se présenter
days.objective: Introduce yourself: name, nationality and where you're from.
days.paretoFocus: Three patterns (Je m'appelle…, Je suis…, Je viens de…) carry every first introduction.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'se présenter' activity.
Say your own full introduction aloud 5 times.
Learn 10 new words in the Words tab.

days.title: Les nombres et l'âge
days.objective: Count and give your age and phone number.
days.paretoFocus: Numbers 0-20 plus the tens unlock prices, times, ages and phone numbers, daily survival.

Count aloud 0-20, then the tens to 60.
Say your age and phone number in French.

days.title: Le genre et les articles
days.objective: Use le/la/un/une correctly and learn nouns with their gender.
days.paretoFocus: Every French noun has a gender shown by its article. Learn each noun WITH le/la, it's the single biggest habit for accuracy.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'articles/gender' activity.
Learn 10 nouns WITH their article in the Words tab.
Label 5 objects at home with le/la.

items: Recall: which article for a feminine noun?
Say 3 nouns with le and 3 with la.
When does le/la become l'?

activities.title: Les articles
activities.intro: Definite (le/la/les) = specific; indefinite (un/une/des) = a/some. Before a vowel, le/la → l'.

activities.title: Articles
activities.intro: Choose the right article.

days.title: Être et avoir
days.objective: Conjugate the two most important verbs in the present.
days.paretoFocus: être (to be) and avoir (to have) describe states, possession and age, and later build the past tense. Memorise them cold.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'être/avoir' activity.
Recite both verbs aloud, all persons.
Write 4 sentences: 2 with être, 2 with avoir.

items: Conjugate être from memory.
Conjugate avoir from memory.
Say 'I'm hungry' and 'I'm tired'.

activities.intro: Irregular but essential. Say the full conjugations aloud.

items.hr: je suis / tu es / il est

items.en: we are / you are / they are
I have / you have / he has

items.hr: nous avons / vous avez / ils ont
items.en: we have / you have / they have
I am hungry. (lit. I have hunger)

days.title: Le présent : -ER, aller, faire
days.objective: Conjugate regular -ER verbs and the key irregulars aller and faire.
days.paretoFocus: Regular -ER verbs are ~90% of all French verbs and share ONE ending pattern. Add aller and faire and you can talk about daily life.
drills: Do a TV5MONDE A1 'present tense' activity.
Conjugate parler, aller, faire aloud.
Write 5 sentences about your day.

items: Conjugate a regular -ER verb from memory.
Recall je vais / nous allons.

activities.title: Le présent
activities.intro: Drop -er, add -e/-es/-e/-ons/-ez/-ent. aller and faire are irregular.

items.hr: je parle / tu parles / il parle
nous parlons / vous parlez / ils parlent
je vais / tu vas / il va / nous allons / vous allez / ils vont
je fais / tu fais / il fait / nous faisons / vous faites / ils font

days.title: Manger et boire
days.objective: Order food and drink and use the partitive du/de la.
days.paretoFocus: The partitive (du/de la/des = 'some') is essential at any café or table, the most practical A1 grammar point.

days.title: La négation et les questions
days.objective: Make negatives with ne…pas and ask questions three ways.
days.paretoFocus: ne…pas and the three question forms let you say what ISN'T and ask about anything, the backbone of conversation.

days.title: Le futur proche
days.objective: Talk about plans with aller + infinitive.

days.objective: Talk about the past with avoir + past participle.

days.title: La routine quotidienne
days.objective: Learn the key reflexive and everyday verbs used to describe your daily routine.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of routine verbs (se lever, se laver, manger, travailler) cover most A1 self-description tasks in the DELF oral.
drills: Say your morning routine aloud with three verbs
Conjugate se lever in je/tu/il
Write one sentence with 'd'abord' and 'ensuite'

items: Recall five daily-routine verbs from memory
Say 'I get up at 7' in French
Order the actions of a typical morning

activities.title: Les verbes de la routine / Daily routine verbs
activities.intro: These verbs let you describe what you do every day. Several are reflexive (with 'se'), so watch the pronoun.

items.hr: Je me lève à sept heures.
items.note: se lever is reflexive: je me lève

items.hr: Je me lave et je m'habille.
items.en: I wash and I get dressed.

items.note: prendre le petit-déjeuner = to have breakfast

items.hr: Le soir, je me couche tôt.
items.en: In the evening, I go to bed early.
items.note: se coucher = to go to bed

activities.intro: Choose or type the correct form. Pay attention to reflexive pronouns.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to go to bed'?
questions.explanation: se coucher = to go to bed.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I get dressed' (use je).

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I have breakfast'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I get up at seven o'clock.'
questions.explanation: Subject + reflexive pronoun + verb + time expression.

days.title: Dire l'heure
days.objective: Learn to ask for and tell the time in French.
days.paretoFocus: Telling the time is tested directly in the DELF A1 and unlocks scheduling, appointments, and daily-routine talk.

activities.title: Quelle heure est-il ? / What time is it?

questions.explanation: Quelle heure est-il ? is the standard question.

days.title: Ma journée

days.paretoFocus: Linking actions to times ('à midi je mange') is exactly the sentence pattern the DELF A1 oral rewards.

items.hr: Il est grand, elle est grande.
items.en: He is tall, she is tall.

items.hr: Il est petit, elle est petite.
items.en: He is short, she is short.

items.hr: Elle a les cheveux longs et bruns.
Elle est belle, il est beau.
items.en: She is beautiful, he is handsome.

activities.title: Accord des adjectifs
activities.intro: Practice agreement and vocabulary of physical description.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'Marie is tall.'
questions.explanation: Marie is feminine singular, so 'grand' becomes 'grande'.

questions.prompt: Which is the feminine of 'beau'?
Complete: 'Elle a les cheveux ___ (long, feminine plural agreement).'
questions.explanation: 'Cheveux' is masculine plural, so the adjective is 'longs'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'He has blue eyes.'
Build the French for: 'She is short and brown-haired.'
questions.explanation: Subject + être + adjectives, all feminine to agree with 'elle'.

activities.title: Tu es comment ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks you to describe a classmate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ton ami Paul, il est comment ?
lines.en: Your friend Paul, what does he look like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est grand et il a les cheveux bruns.
lines.en: He is tall and he has brown hair.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And his eyes?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: He has green eyes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Is he handsome?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il est beau et sympa.
lines.en: Yes, he is handsome and nice.

days.title: Le caractère
days.objective: Describe someone's personality using common character adjectives with agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'être' + personality adjectives and connect them with 'mais' and 'et'.
drills: Describe two family members' personalities with two adjectives each.
Make five sentences contrasting traits using 'mais'.
Convert five personality adjectives to the feminine.

items: Recall the feminine of sérieux, gentil, timide, sportif.
Say three adjectives that describe you.
Give the opposite of 'sympa' and 'calme'.

activities.title: Adjectifs de caractère
activities.intro: Common personality adjectives with feminine forms.

items.hr: Il est gentil, elle est gentille.
items.en: He is kind, she is kind.

items.hr: Il est sérieux, elle est sérieuse.
items.en: He is serious, she is serious.

items.hr: Il est sympa mais un peu paresseux.
items.en: He is nice but a bit lazy.

activities.title: Décrire le caractère
activities.intro: Practice personality adjectives and their agreement.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'She is kind.'
Which word means 'shy'?
Complete: 'Marie est ___ (serious, feminine).'
Type the French for 'He is nice but lazy.'
Build the French for: 'She is intelligent and kind.'
questions.explanation: Both adjectives take the feminine form to agree with 'elle'.

activities.title: Comment est ta sœur ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about your sister's personality.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What is your sister like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: She is kind and funny.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Is she shy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, elle n'est pas timide, elle est sociable.
lines.en: No, she is not shy, she is sociable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And is she serious?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, elle est sérieuse et travailleuse.
lines.en: Yes, she is serious and hardworking.

days.title: Le temps qu'il fait
days.objective: Talk about the weather using impersonal expressions with 'il fait' and 'il y a'.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'Il fait...', 'Il y a...', and 'Il pleut/neige' to describe weather.
drills: Describe today's weather in three sentences.
Match five weather expressions to their English meaning.
Ask and answer 'Quel temps fait-il ?' for four cities.

items: Recall the weather verbs pleuvoir and neiger in 'il' form.
Say four things using 'Il fait...'.
Give the question 'What's the weather like?' in French.

activities.title: La météo
activities.intro: Impersonal weather expressions used with 'il'.

activities.title: Quel temps fait-il ?
activities.intro: Practice weather expressions.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'It is raining'?
Which expression means 'It is windy'?
questions.explanation: Wind uses 'il y a du vent'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'En hiver, il ___ froid.'
Type the French for 'It is snowing.'
Build the French for: 'Today the weather is nice.'

activities.title: La météo aujourd'hui
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about the weather where you are.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What's the weather like where you are?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il fait froid et il pleut.
lines.en: It is cold and it is raining.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Is it windy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il y a beaucoup de vent.
lines.en: Yes, it is very windy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Tomorrow, the weather is going to be nice.

days.title: Les saisons
days.objective: Name the four seasons and say what activities you do in each.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'en été/automne/hiver' and 'au printemps' plus common seasonal activities.
drills: Say one activity you do in each season.
Match each season to typical weather.
Write three sentences with 'en été je...' style.

items: Recall the four seasons in order.
Remember which season uses 'au' (printemps).
Say two summer and two winter activities.

activities.title: Les quatre saisons
activities.intro: The seasons and how to say 'in' each one.

items.hr: En été, je vais à la plage.
items.en: In summer, I go to the beach.

activities.title: Saisons et activités
activities.intro: Practice seasons and seasonal activities.

questions.prompt: Which is correct for 'in spring'?
questions.explanation: Spring is the exception and uses 'au printemps'.

questions.prompt: Which season is 'l'hiver'?
Complete: '___ été, il fait chaud.'
Type the French for 'In winter, I ski.'
questions.answer: En hiver, je fais du ski.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In summer I go to the beach.'

activities.title: Ta saison préférée
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about your favourite season.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What is your favourite season?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: My favourite season is summer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Why?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En été, il fait beau et je vais à la plage.
lines.en: In summer, the weather is nice and I go to the beach.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And in winter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En hiver, je fais du ski avec ma famille.
lines.en: In winter, I ski with my family.

days.title: Le corps
days.objective: Name the main parts of the body with correct articles.
days.paretoFocus: Learn body-part nouns with 'le/la/les' and the structure 'j'ai...'.
drills: Point to five body parts and name them in French.
Sort ten body parts by gender (le/la).
Say 'J'ai deux...' for parts that come in pairs.

items: Recall the articles for tête, main, bras, jambe.
Name the five senses' organs (eyes, ears, nose...).
List body parts from head to foot.

activities.title: Les parties du corps
activities.intro: Main body parts with their articles.

activities.title: Nommer le corps
activities.intro: Practice body-part vocabulary and articles.

questions.prompt: What is 'the head' in French?
Which word means 'the hand'?
Complete with the article: '___ bras' (the arm).
Type the French for 'the feet' (plural).
Build the French for: 'I have a headache.'
questions.explanation: 'avoir mal à' + body part expresses pain.

activities.title: Le corps en cours
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A teacher does a body-parts warm-up with you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Show me your head.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Here it is, this is the head.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And your hands?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: I have two hands.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: How many legs do you have?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai deux jambes et deux pieds.
lines.en: I have two legs and two feet.

days.title: Chez le médecin
days.objective: Explain what hurts at the doctor's using 'avoir mal à'.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'avoir mal à' with contractions (au, à la, à l', aux) plus basic doctor phrases.
drills: Say five sentences with 'J'ai mal à/au/aux...'.
Practice the contractions of 'à + le/la/les' with body parts.
Role-play describing three symptoms.

items: Recall 'à + le = au' and 'à + les = aux'.
Say 'I have a headache' and 'I have a stomachache'.
Remember the phrase 'Où avez-vous mal ?'.

activities.title: Avoir mal à
activities.intro: Expressing pain with the correct contraction of 'à'.

activities.title: Où avez-vous mal ?
activities.intro: Practice pain expressions and contractions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I have a stomachache.'
Which is correct: 'I have a toothache'?
Complete: 'J'ai mal ___ gorge.' (throat)
questions.explanation: 'Gorge' is feminine, so 'à la gorge' with no contraction.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I am sick.'
Build the French for: 'I have a stomach ache.'

activities.title: Une visite chez le médecin
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. You visit the doctor because you feel unwell.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Hello, where does it hurt?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour docteur, j'ai mal à la tête.
lines.en: Hello doctor, I have a headache.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Do you have a fever?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et j'ai mal à la gorge.
lines.en: Yes, and I have a sore throat.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: You are sick. Rest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Thank you very much, doctor.

days.title: Les émotions
days.objective: Express feelings and physical states using 'avoir' expressions.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'avoir peur/faim/froid/chaud/soif' and 'être content/triste'.
drills: Say five states with 'J'ai...' (faim, soif, froid...).
Contrast 'avoir' expressions with 'être content/triste'.
Describe how you feel in three situations.

items: Recall which feelings use 'avoir' vs 'être'.
Say 'I am hungry', 'I am cold', 'I am afraid'.
Give the feminine of 'content' and 'heureux'.

activities.title: Avoir et les émotions
activities.intro: Feelings and states, many using 'avoir' where English uses 'to be'.

items.hr: Je suis content, je suis contente.

activities.title: Exprimer ses émotions
activities.intro: Practice avoir/être feeling expressions.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I am hungry'?
Which uses 'être' correctly?
Complete: 'J'ai ___.' (I am afraid)
Type the French for 'She is happy.' (feminine)
Build the French for: 'I'm cold and hungry.'
questions.explanation: Both states share one 'avoir', linked by 'et'.

activities.title: Comment tu te sens ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend checks how you are feeling.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Are you okay? Are you hungry?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai faim et j'ai soif.
lines.en: Yes, I am hungry and thirsty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Are you cold too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, mais je suis un peu fatigué.
lines.en: No, but I am a bit tired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Are you happy today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Yes, I am very happy!

days.title: Les adjectifs possessifs : mon, ma, mes
days.objective: You can say who something or someone belongs to using mon, ma, mes and the matching forms for 'your'.
days.paretoFocus: Possessives appear in almost every personal sentence you will ever say, from introducing your family to pointing at your bag.
drills: Walk around your home and name ten objects out loud with mon, ma or mes, checking the gender each time.
Write five sentences introducing members of your family, using a different possessive form in each one.
Take five sentences you wrote with mon or ma and say them again about the other person, switching to ton or ta.

items: Which possessive goes before a plural noun, whatever its gender?
Why do the French say mon amie and not ma amie?
Say three things you own: one masculine, one feminine, one plural.

activities.title: Mon, ma, mes
activities.intro: In French the possessive agrees with the thing owned, not with the owner. Use mon before a masculine noun, ma before a feminine noun, and mes before any plural. Before a feminine noun that starts with a vowel sound, French uses mon so that two vowels do not meet.

items.note: amie is feminine but begins with a vowel, so mon

items.hr: Ton livre est sur ma table.
items.en: Your book is on my table.
items.note: ton, ta, tes work the same way for 'your'

activities.title: À qui est-ce ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing the possessive that matches the noun that follows it.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form for 'my': '___ voiture est bleue.'
questions.explanation: Voiture is feminine and singular, so the possessive is ma.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct French?
questions.explanation: Amie is feminine, but it begins with a vowel sound, so French uses mon to keep the words easy to pronounce.

questions.prompt: Complete with the possessive meaning 'my': '___ chiens sont dans le jardin.'
questions.explanation: Chiens is plural, and every plural noun takes mes whatever its gender.

questions.prompt: Complete with the informal possessive meaning 'your': '___ mère travaille ici.'
questions.explanation: Mère is feminine and singular, so the informal form of 'your' is ta.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'My sister lives with my mum and dad.'
questions.explanation: The possessive agrees with the noun owned: ma before the feminine sœur, mes before the plural parents.

activities.title: La photo de famille
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend looks at a family photo on your phone and you explain who everyone is, using possessives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oh, c'est ta famille sur cette photo ?
lines.en: Oh, is that your family in this photo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ce sont mes parents et ma sœur.
lines.en: Yes, those are my parents and my sister.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le garçon à gauche, c'est ton frère ?
lines.en: And the boy on the left, is that your brother?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, c'est mon cousin. Il s'appelle Hugo.
lines.en: No, that's my cousin. His name is Hugo.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ta sœur ressemble beaucoup à ta mère.
lines.en: Your sister looks a lot like your mother.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai. Moi, j'ai les yeux de mon père.
lines.en: That's true. Me, I have my father's eyes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ton chien ? Il est où ?
lines.en: And your dog? Where is he?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mon chien est chez mes grands-parents.
lines.en: My dog is at my grandparents' house.

days.title: Les démonstratifs : ce, cette, ces et le choix entre « c'est » et « il est »
days.objective: You can point at things with ce, cet, cette and ces, and choose correctly between c'est and il est or elle est.
days.paretoFocus: Pointing at something in front of you and then saying what it is or what it is like covers most shop, street and restaurant conversations.
drills: Point at ten objects around you and say a sentence with ce, cet, cette or ces before each one.
Write five pairs of sentences: first identify a person with c'est, then describe them with il est or elle est.
Say aloud five things you can see right now, each time adding whether it is big, small, new or old.

items: When do you use cet instead of ce?
Which form comes before an article plus a noun, c'est or il est?
Give one sentence that identifies a person and one that describes them.

activities.title: Ce, cette, ces
activities.intro: Ce, cette and ces mean 'this, that, these, those'. Use ce with a masculine noun, cet before a masculine noun that starts with a vowel sound, cette with a feminine noun, and ces with every plural. To identify something use c'est plus an article and a noun; to describe it use il est or elle est plus an adjective alone.

items.hr: Ce train part à huit heures.

items.note: cet before a masculine noun beginning with a vowel sound

items.hr: Il est italien et il est très gentil.
items.en: He is Italian and he is very kind.
items.note: il est plus an adjective, with no article

activities.title: Ce ou cette ?
activities.intro: Practise the demonstratives and the difference between identifying and describing.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct demonstrative: '___ hôtel est complet.'
questions.explanation: Hôtel is masculine and starts with a silent h, so the special form cet is used before the vowel sound.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses c'est and elle est correctly?
options: C'est gentil, elle est une amie.
Elle est une amie et c'est gentille.
C'est une amie et elle est gentille.
Il est une amie et elle est gentille.
questions.answer: C'est une amie et elle est gentille.
questions.explanation: C'est comes before an article plus a noun, while elle est comes before an adjective on its own.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right demonstrative: '___ chaussures sont trop petites.'
questions.explanation: Chaussures is plural, and ces is used before every plural noun.

questions.prompt: Complete the identification: '___ ma voisine, elle travaille à la banque.'
questions.explanation: Before a possessive plus a noun, French identifies with c'est and not with elle est.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This shop is closed today.'
questions.explanation: Magasin is masculine and singular, so it takes the demonstrative ce, and the adjective fermé agrees with it.

activities.title: Dans la rue commerçante
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You walk past shops with a friend and comment on what you see, using the demonstratives and c'est.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Look, this shop is new.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, c'est une boulangerie. Elle est très jolie.
lines.en: Yes, it is a bakery. It is very pretty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ce café, tu le connais ?
lines.en: And this cafe, do you know it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, c'est mon café préféré. Il est calme le matin.
lines.en: Of course, it is my favourite cafe. It is quiet in the morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ces gâteaux dans la vitrine sont magnifiques.
lines.en: These cakes in the window are magnificent.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai. Cet éclair au chocolat est délicieux.
lines.en: That is true. This chocolate eclair is delicious.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, on entre dans ce café ?
lines.en: So, shall we go into this cafe?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: All right, that is a good idea.

days.title: Les prépositions de lieu : sur, sous, devant, derrière, entre
days.objective: You can say exactly where an object or a place is using sur, sous, devant, derrière and entre.
days.paretoFocus: These five words let you find lost objects, follow instructions and describe any room or street without knowing much other vocabulary.
drills: Put four small objects on your desk and describe their positions out loud in five sentences.
Describe the shop or building next to where you live, saying what is in front of it and behind it.
Hide an object, then explain to an imaginary friend where it is in three different ways.

items: Which word do you use for something placed on top of another thing?
Which small word joins the two places named after entre?
Describe the position of your bag right now in one full sentence.

activities.title: Où sont les choses ?
activities.intro: These short words say where something is. Sur means on, sous means under, devant means in front of, derrière means behind, and entre means between. They come straight before the noun with its article, and they are usually used after être or after il y a.

items.hr: Le livre est sur la table.
items.en: The book is on the table.

items.hr: Le chat dort sous le lit.
items.en: The cat is sleeping under the bed.

items.hr: Il y a un arbre devant la maison.
items.en: There is a tree in front of the house.

items.hr: Le parking est derrière le supermarché.
items.en: The car park is behind the supermarket.

items.hr: La pharmacie est entre la banque et la poste.
items.en: The chemist is between the bank and the post office.
items.note: entre links the two places with et

activities.title: Sur, sous ou entre ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing the preposition that gives the right position.

questions.prompt: Your keys are lying on top of your bag. Which sentence says this?
options: Mes clés sont sous mon sac.
Mes clés sont sur mon sac.
Mes clés sont derrière mon sac.
Mes clés sont devant mon sac.
questions.answer: Mes clés sont sur mon sac.
questions.explanation: Sur means on or on top of, while sous would place the keys underneath the bag.

questions.prompt: Choose the preposition: 'Le chien est ___ la voiture, on ne le voit pas.'
questions.explanation: If the dog cannot be seen from the front, it is behind the car, and behind is derrière.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Mon appartement est ___ la boulangerie et le cinéma.'
questions.explanation: Two places joined by et need the preposition meaning between.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le bus s'arrête ___ la gare, juste à l'entrée.'
questions.explanation: Stopping right at the entrance means the bus stops in front of the station.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'There is a cat under the chair.'
questions.explanation: Il y a introduces the existence of something, and sous plus the article gives its position.

activities.title: Je cherche mes clés
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You cannot find your keys and your flatmate suggests places to look, using prepositions of place.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Are you looking for something?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je ne trouve pas mes clés.
lines.en: Yes, I can't find my keys.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elles sont peut-être sur la table de la cuisine.
lines.en: Maybe they are on the kitchen table.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, j'ai regardé. Elles ne sont pas sur la table.
lines.en: No, I looked. They are not on the table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et sous le canapé ? Ou derrière la porte ?
lines.en: And under the sofa? Or behind the door?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attends... Elles sont entre les coussins du canapé !
lines.en: Wait... They are between the sofa cushions!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait. Et ton sac est devant l'entrée.
lines.en: Perfect. And your bag is in front of the door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci beaucoup. Je pars tout de suite.
lines.en: Thank you very much. I am leaving right away.

days.title: Les verbes pronominaux au présent : se lever, se laver, s'appeler
days.objective: You can conjugate reflexive verbs in the present and place the reflexive pronoun correctly, including in the negative.
days.paretoFocus: A large share of everyday French verbs are reflexive, so this one pattern unlocks getting up, washing, dressing, resting and introducing yourself.
drills: Conjugate se lever aloud through all six persons, then do the same with se laver.
Write five sentences about your morning, each with a different reflexive verb.
Turn three of those sentences into the negative, keeping the pronoun in the right place.

items: Where does the reflexive pronoun go in a present tense sentence?
What happens to me, te and se before a verb starting with a vowel?
Say what time you get up and what time you go to bed.

activities.title: Les verbes pronominaux
activities.intro: Some French verbs carry a small pronoun that points back to the subject: je me, tu te, il ou elle se, nous nous, vous vous, ils ou elles se. The pronoun sits between the subject and the verb. Me, te and se become m', t' and s' in front of a vowel.

items.hr: Je me lève à sept heures.
Tu te laves les mains avant de manger.
items.en: You wash your hands before eating.
items.note: with body parts French uses le, la, les and not the possessive
se becomes s' before the vowel of appelle

items.hr: Nous nous couchons tard le samedi.
items.en: We go to bed late on Saturdays.

items.note: in the negative, ne comes before the pronoun

activities.title: Je me lève, tu te lèves
activities.intro: Practise reflexive pronouns and the present tense forms that go with them.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'Nous ___ levons à six heures.'
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun always matches the subject, so a nous subject takes nous.

questions.prompt: Which sentence gives your name correctly?
questions.explanation: Me loses its vowel and becomes m' in front of the verb appelle.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing pronoun: 'Elle ___ lave le visage le matin.'
questions.explanation: A third person singular subject takes the reflexive pronoun se, placed before the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb meaning 'get up': 'Vous vous ___ à quelle heure le dimanche ?'
questions.explanation: With vous the verb keeps the plain stem lev and adds the ending ez, so there is no accent.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I get up early every day.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun me sits between the subject and the verb, and lève takes a grave accent in the singular.

activities.title: Le matin à la maison
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A guest asks about your morning habits and you answer with reflexive verbs in the present.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu te lèves à quelle heure en semaine ?
lines.en: What time do you get up during the week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me lève à six heures et demie.
lines.en: I get up at half past six.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est tôt ! Et tu te douches le matin ?
lines.en: That is early! And do you shower in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je me douche puis je m'habille vite.
lines.en: Yes, I shower and then I get dressed quickly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ton frère ? Il se réveille aussi tôt ?
lines.en: And your brother? Does he wake up that early too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, lui, il se réveille à huit heures.
lines.en: No, he wakes up at eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le week-end, vous vous reposez un peu ?
lines.en: And at the weekend, do you rest a little?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, nous nous levons vers dix heures.
lines.en: Yes, we get up around ten.

days.title: Pouvoir, vouloir, devoir : les verbes modaux du quotidien
days.objective: You can express ability, wishes and obligation with pouvoir, vouloir and devoir followed by an infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: These three verbs turn a small stock of infinitives into hundreds of useful sentences, and they carry almost every request you will make.
drills: Write three things you can do today, three you want to do and three you have to do.
Say five polite requests aloud, each starting with the softened form of vouloir.
Take three sentences with devoir and make them negative, keeping the infinitive at the end.

items: What form does the verb after pouvoir, vouloir or devoir always take?
Which form of vouloir sounds polite in a shop or an office?
Give the ils form of devoir.

activities.title: Pouvoir, vouloir, devoir
activities.intro: These three irregular verbs express ability, wish and obligation. The verb that follows them is always an infinitive, with nothing in between. Je veux sounds blunt, so for a request French speakers prefer the softer je voudrais.

items.note: vouloir can also take a noun directly

items.hr: Vous pouvez répéter, s'il vous plaît ?
Je voudrais deux tickets, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: I would like two tickets, please.
items.note: voudrais is the polite form of je veux

activities.title: Je peux, je veux, je dois
activities.intro: Practise the forms of the three modal verbs and the meaning each one carries.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ils ___ finir le travail avant midi.'
questions.explanation: The third person plural of devoir is doivent, and the verb after it stays in the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses an obligation?
questions.explanation: Devoir expresses obligation, while pouvoir expresses ability and vouloir expresses a wish.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right form of pouvoir: 'Est-ce que tu ___ m'aider ?'
questions.explanation: With tu, pouvoir becomes peux, and the following verb aider stays in the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Make this request polite by changing the verb of 'Je veux un renseignement' : 'Je ___ un renseignement.'
questions.explanation: The conditional form of vouloir softens a request and sounds far more polite than the plain present.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We must not forget the tickets.'
questions.explanation: In the negative, ne and pas surround the conjugated verb devons, and oublier stays in the infinitive.

activities.title: Un service à demander
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks you for a favour and you explain what you can, want and must do.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Can you help me for five minutes?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je voudrais bien, mais je dois finir ce rapport.
lines.en: I would like to, but I have to finish this report.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, tu peux venir demain matin ?
lines.en: So, can you come tomorrow morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, demain je peux. Je suis libre après neuf heures.
lines.en: Yes, tomorrow I can. I am free after nine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait. On doit aussi appeler le client.
lines.en: Perfect. We also have to call the client.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Je peux l'appeler cet après-midi.
lines.en: All right. I can call him this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super. Tu veux un café avant de partir ?
lines.en: Great. Do you want a coffee before you leave?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non merci, je dois vraiment travailler maintenant.
lines.en: No thanks, I really have to work now.

days.title: L'impératif : donner une instruction ou un conseil
days.objective: You can give and refuse instructions using the tu, nous and vous forms of the imperative.
days.paretoFocus: Instructions are everywhere in real life, from recipes and directions to signs and advice, and the form is simply the present tense without the subject.
drills: Give five instructions to a friend using tu forms, then repeat the same five with vous.
Write three warnings in the negative, putting ne before the verb and pas after it.
Explain a simple task you do often in four imperative steps.

items: What happens to the final s of an -er verb in the tu imperative?
What does the nous form of the imperative mean in English?
Give the vous imperative of être.

activities.title: L'impératif
activities.intro: To give an instruction, drop the subject pronoun and keep only the verb, in the tu, nous or vous form. With verbs that end in er, the tu form loses its final s, so tu parles becomes parle. To tell someone not to do something, put ne before the verb and pas after it.

items.hr: Ferme la porte, s'il te plaît.
items.note: tu form of fermer, with no final s
vous form, for a group or for politeness

items.hr: Sois patient et prends ton temps.
items.en: Be patient and take your time.
items.note: être is irregular: sois, soyons, soyez

activities.title: Donne, faites, allons
activities.intro: Practise forming instructions in the three imperative persons.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct instruction to a friend: '___ la fenêtre, il fait chaud.'
questions.explanation: Ouvrir takes er endings in the present, so its tu imperative drops the final s and loses the subject.

questions.prompt: Which sentence tells a group not to run?
questions.explanation: A group takes the vous form, and ne goes before the verb with pas straight after it.

questions.prompt: Give the instruction to a friend meaning 'wait': '___ deux minutes !'
questions.explanation: Only er type verbs drop the final s, so this verb keeps its s in the tu imperative.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite advice with the imperative of être: '___ prudent sur la route.'
questions.explanation: Être is irregular in the imperative and its polite vous form is soyez.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Let's take the underground and go to the station.'
questions.explanation: The nous imperative drops the subject and means let's, which gives prenons and allons.

activities.title: Une recette simple
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain a simple recipe to a friend step by step, using the imperative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je voudrais faire une omelette. Explique-moi.
lines.en: I would like to make an omelette. Explain it to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Casse trois œufs dans un bol.
lines.en: All right. Break three eggs into a bowl.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et après ? Je mets du lait ?
lines.en: And then? Do I add milk?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, n'ajoute pas de lait. Mets juste un peu de sel.
lines.en: No, do not add milk. Just put in a little salt.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien. Et pour la cuisson ?
lines.en: Very good. And for the cooking?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chauffe la poêle, puis verse les œufs.
lines.en: Heat the pan, then pour in the eggs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: For how long?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attends deux minutes et ne remue pas trop.
lines.en: Wait two minutes and do not stir too much.

days.title: Inviter, accepter et refuser poliment
days.objective: You can invite someone out, accept an invitation warmly and turn one down without sounding rude.
days.paretoFocus: Invitations are how social life starts, and a bare non damages a relationship, so the polite formulas matter as much as the grammar.
drills: Write three invitations for three different activities, each in a different way.
Practise two warm acceptances and two polite refusals out loud until they come automatically.
Refuse an invitation in writing, then add a sentence proposing another moment.

items: Which everyday expression uses the verb dire to invite a friend?
Name two short ways of accepting an invitation enthusiastically.
What two things does a polite refusal contain besides the word no?

activities.title: Inviter et répondre
activities.intro: To invite someone, French uses a simple present tense question or a set expression such as ça te dit de plus an infinitive. To accept, say avec plaisir or volontiers. To refuse, thank the person, give a short reason, and offer another moment, because a bare non sounds cold.

items.hr: Ça te dit d'aller au cinéma samedi ?
items.en: Do you fancy going to the cinema on Saturday?
items.note: ça te dit de plus an infinitive, very common among friends

items.en: Are you free on Friday evening?

items.hr: C'est gentil, mais je ne peux pas, je travaille.
items.en: That is kind, but I can't, I am working.

items.note: a soft way to keep the door open after refusing

activities.title: Volontiers ou désolé ?
activities.intro: Practise the phrases used to invite, to accept and to refuse.

questions.prompt: A friend invites you out and you want to accept warmly. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Volontiers is a short enthusiastic yes to an invitation or an offer.

questions.prompt: Which reply turns down an invitation politely?
options: Désolé, je ne suis pas libre ce soir.
questions.answer: Désolé, je ne suis pas libre ce soir.
questions.explanation: A polite refusal apologises and gives a reason instead of a flat no.

questions.prompt: Complete the invitation: 'Ça te ___ d'aller à la piscine demain ?'
questions.explanation: The set expression uses the verb dire in the third person singular, followed by de and an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete the enthusiastic acceptance: 'Avec ___ ! J'adore ce restaurant.'
questions.explanation: Avec plaisir is the standard way of accepting an invitation or an offer.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Are you free on Saturday evening?'
questions.explanation: Est-ce que turns a statement into a question without changing the word order after it.

activities.title: Une invitation pour samedi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend invites you out twice; you refuse the first plan politely and accept the second.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça te dit d'aller au concert vendredi soir ?
lines.en: Do you fancy going to the concert on Friday evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est gentil, mais vendredi je travaille tard.
lines.en: That is kind, but on Friday I am working late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dommage. Et samedi, tu es libre ?
lines.en: What a pity. And on Saturday, are you free?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Samedi, oui, avec plaisir ! On fait quoi ?
lines.en: Saturday, yes, with pleasure! What shall we do?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On peut dîner en ville, puis boire un verre.
lines.en: We can have dinner in town, then have a drink.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volontiers. On se retrouve à quelle heure ?
lines.en: Gladly. What time shall we meet?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vers sept heures devant le théâtre, ça va ?
lines.en: Around seven in front of the theatre, is that all right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Perfect, see you on Saturday then!

days.title: À l'école et à l'université : parler de ses études
days.objective: You can say what you study, where you study and what your timetable looks like.
days.paretoFocus: Studies come up in the first minutes of almost every conversation with a new person your age, and the vocabulary is fixed and reusable.
drills: Write four sentences about what you study or studied, using étudier, faire des études and apprendre.
Describe your week of classes in three sentences, giving days and times.
Say aloud three things you need before an exam and what you do to prepare.

items: Which expression uses faire and de to name your field of study?
What is the difference between un cours and une classe?
Say how long you have been learning French, using depuis.

activities.title: Mes études
activities.intro: To talk about studies, French uses étudier plus a subject, faire des études de plus a field, and apprendre plus a language or a skill. School subjects normally keep the definite article, as in j'étudie les maths. In everyday speech la fac is the usual short word for university.

items.note: étudier plus the article plus the subject

items.hr: Elle est en deuxième année de médecine.
items.en: She is in the second year of medicine.

items.hr: Mon cours de maths commence à neuf heures.
items.en: My maths class starts at nine.

items.hr: J'apprends le français depuis six mois.
items.en: I have been learning French for six months.
items.note: depuis with the present tense for something still going on

items.hr: J'ai un examen la semaine prochaine.
items.en: I have an exam next week.

activities.title: Qu'est-ce que tu étudies ?
activities.intro: Practise the expressions used to talk about studies and classes.

questions.prompt: Choose the natural way to name your field of study.
options: Je fais des études de biologie.
Je fais une étude de biologie.
questions.answer: Je fais des études de biologie.
questions.explanation: The fixed expression is faire des études de plus the field, with no article after de.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: 'Mon ___ d'histoire est très intéressant.'
questions.explanation: Un cours is the class you attend in a subject, while une classe is the group of pupils or the room.

questions.prompt: Complete with the contracted preposition: 'Je vais ___ cours de chimie le mardi.'
questions.explanation: À plus le contracts into a single word before a masculine noun such as cours.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right form of apprendre: 'Nous ___ l'espagnol cette année.'
questions.explanation: Apprendre follows the pattern of prendre, so the nous form is apprenons.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have a chemistry test on Monday.'
questions.explanation: The subject of an exam is introduced by de, and a day used on its own needs no preposition.

activities.title: À la cafétéria de la fac
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet another student and you talk about what you study and how busy your week is.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Salut ! Tu étudies quoi cette année ?
lines.en: Hi! What are you studying this year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'étudie l'économie. Je suis en première année.
lines.en: I study economics. I am in the first year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je fais des études de langues. J'apprends le russe.
lines.en: I am doing language studies. I am learning Russian.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est difficile ? Tu as beaucoup de cours ?
lines.en: Is it difficult? Do you have a lot of classes?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, quinze heures par semaine, plus le travail à la maison.
lines.en: Yes, fifteen hours a week, plus the work at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Moi, j'ai un examen important vendredi.
lines.en: I have an important exam on Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon courage ! Tu révises à la bibliothèque ?
lines.en: Good luck! Do you revise at the library?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je révise là-bas tous les soirs après les cours.
lines.en: Yes, I revise there every evening after classes.

days.title: Les animaux et la nature
days.objective: You can name common animals and natural places and say where you go and what you see outdoors.
days.paretoFocus: Pets and weekend plans in the countryside are constant small talk topics, and these nouns recycle every article and preposition you already know.
drills: List eight animals out loud with the correct article, then say the plural of each one.
Write four sentences about a walk outdoors, using il y a and a preposition of place.
Describe where you like to go in each season, using à la campagne, à la montagne or au bord de la mer.

items: What is the plural of cheval?
Which verb do you use to say you own a pet?
Name three places in nature and the little words that go before them.

activities.title: Les animaux et la nature
activities.intro: Animal names take un or une like any other noun, and you say you own one with avoir. Two plurals are worth learning by heart: cheval becomes chevaux and oiseau becomes oiseaux. For natural places French says à la campagne, à la montagne and au bord de la mer.

items.hr: J'ai un chat et deux oiseaux.
items.en: I have a cat and two birds.

items.hr: Il y a des vaches et des chevaux dans le champ.
items.en: There are cows and horses in the field.
items.note: cheval becomes chevaux in the plural

items.hr: Nous marchons dans la forêt le dimanche.
items.en: We walk in the forest on Sundays.

items.hr: La rivière passe derrière le village.
items.en: The river runs behind the village.

items.hr: En été, je vais à la montagne.
items.en: In summer, I go to the mountains.
items.note: à la campagne, à la montagne, au bord de la mer

activities.title: Dans la forêt et dans les champs
activities.intro: Practise animal nouns, their plurals and the expressions for natural places.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct plural: 'Il y a trois ___ dans le pré.'
questions.explanation: Cheval forms its plural in aux, and cheveux is a different word that means hair.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says that you own a dog?
options: Il y a moi un chien.
questions.explanation: Owning an animal uses avoir, so je suis un chien would mean that you are a dog.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for the countryside: 'Le week-end, nous allons à la ___ pour respirer l'air pur.'
questions.explanation: The countryside is feminine in French, so it follows à la in expressions of destination.

questions.prompt: Complete with the plural word for birds: 'Les ___ chantent dans le jardin le matin.'
questions.explanation: The singular ends in eau, and nouns of that type add an x rather than an s in the plural.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In summer we go to the seaside.'
questions.explanation: Au bord de la mer is the fixed expression for the seaside, with au coming from à plus le.

activities.title: Le week-end à la campagne
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your weekend plans and you describe the animals and the landscape you will find there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Where are you going this weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais à la campagne, chez mes grands-parents.
lines.en: I am going to the countryside, to my grandparents' house.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Do they have animals?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ils ont des poules, deux chèvres et un vieux chien.
lines.en: Yes, they have hens, two goats and an old dog.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Is it pretty there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il y a une forêt derrière la maison et une petite rivière.
lines.en: There is a forest behind the house and a small river.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: So you can go for walks?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je me lève tôt et je marche dans les champs.
lines.en: Yes, I get up early and I walk in the fields.

days.title: Bilan A1
days.objective: Review core A1 skills: greetings, présent, passé composé, and futur proche.
days.paretoFocus: Recognize when to use présent, passé composé, and futur proche in simple sentences.
drills: Give one sentence each in présent, passé composé, and futur proche.
Greet and introduce yourself in four sentences.
Conjugate 'aller' and 'avoir' in the present out loud.

items: Recall the passé composé formula (avoir/être + participe passé).
Recall the futur proche formula (aller + infinitif).
Say a full self-introduction from memory.

activities.title: Les trois temps de base
activities.intro: A quick recap of the three A1 tenses and greetings.

items.en: I am going to eat an apple.

activities.title: Réviser les temps
activities.intro: Mixed review of the three tenses and greetings.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is in the futur proche?
Which auxiliary does 'aller' use in the passé composé?
Complete the passé composé: 'J'___ mangé.' (auxiliary)
Type the French for 'I am going to travel.' (futur proche)
Build the French for: 'I went to the cinema.'
questions.explanation: Passé composé of 'aller' with 'être' + past participle 'allé'.

activities.title: Bilan : parler de soi
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. You review greetings and the three tenses with a partner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Hello! What is your name?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Hello, my name is Alex.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait hier ?
lines.en: What did you do yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Yesterday, I visited a museum.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Demain, je vais aller à la plage.
lines.en: Tomorrow, I am going to go to the beach.
